user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.

Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs. Calls to these services would have experienced higher latency or elevated errors in the form of HTTP 502 response codes.

Customers might face connectivity issues. Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency. Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4.
Increased error rate

Affected customers may see errors when trying to query their monitoring data. Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data. Customers may have also experienced false alerts during the impact window.
Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.
APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may ended up as a loading failure.
UNKNOWN
Google Compute Engine customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss in southamerica-east1-[a, b, c]
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Service was unavailable. Affected customers experienced elevated errors.

Affected customers encountered HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors when accessing various Google Workspace services. Google Cloud customers experienced elevated API traffic error rates, elevated latency, operation deadline expirations, and error messages stating Policy Checks are unavailable.
Visible, near 100% packet loss on Cloud Interconnect.
Some queries may exhibit higher than normal latencies during execution. Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.
Log entries written in europe-west3 are delayed by 1+ hours. They may not show up in Log Explorer or other export destinations.

Google Cloud customers using VMWare Engine experienced connectivity issues while using vSphere, vCenter and NSX. Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs. Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines.
Logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.
Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer. CNAME chasing between private zone to private zone is not working.
new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates
HTTP load balancing returning error for some user in Europe. Increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for GCLB traffic served from HTTPS load balancers in Western Europe.
Users experiencing failure with file uploads
Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running 'tail' commands

Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption. All cloud customers communicating outside the region (including to the internet) would have seen two periods of disruption.
Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts. Containers within GKE cluster node pools using docker are getting restarted in the event of docker restarts.

customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.
Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error.
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service.
Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.

Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building. Customers were unable to create PD devices in the impacted zone. Some customers experienced HTTP 500 errors, dataset unavailability, high error rates, service unavailability and elevated latency.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who migrate to Cloud SQL for MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication using managed dumps may experience errors if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Furthermore, if updates were made to those jobs during the replication phase, the migration job may fail.
Customers impacted by this issue may see high latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors
Connectivity issues in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency
users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer. CNAME chasing between private zone to private zone is not working.

customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. It is possible for customers to experience service interruption in none, one, or both zones. Connections to and from VMs in us-east1-c and us-east1-d may fail.
Customers are unable to login to their admin workstation in both their prod and non-prod environments.
Customers will see a 'Failed to load' error with following message: 'There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project= Please try again'
Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.
Some customers may be experiencing connection failures in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error.
Users might experience higher latency.
We are investigating errors with creating and deleting Cloud Composer environments.
GCE Networking in us-east4 region affecting GCE VMs, GKE, Cloud VPN and Cloud Private Interconnect resulting in network packet loss
Google BigQuery API returning 503 errors, High error rate on multiple Google BigQuery APIs
Node pool creation failing in multiple zones
Networking issues in us-central1-c impacting multiple GCP products (Bigtable, Cloud SQL, Datastore, VMs)
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
StackDriver Trace API is experiencing an elevated rate of request failures.
UNKNOWN
UNKNOWN
Cloud SQL First Generation instances unavailable in us-central1-a
We are investigating increased packet loss into and out of southamerica-east1
Google Kubernetes Engine Node Pool creation through Cloud Console UI
Google Stackdriver Alerts not firing reliably
all GKE API requests in region europe-west4 are failing
Creation of new google cloud functions and update of current one are failing
UNKNOWN
prevents the creation of GCE instances that require allocation of new external IP addresses; Allocation of new external IP addresses for GCE instance creation continues to be unavailable
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
intermittent traffic disruption to and from us-central
The Google Compute Engine service is experiencing delays in network programming in us-east1.
We are investigating timeouts with Google Cloud Functions deployments.
Users may see slow performance or intermittent errors

Google Cloud Storage started experiencing degraded availability for some objects based in us-central1 buckets (Regional, Nearline, Coldline, Durable Reduced Availability) and US multi-region buckets. Between 08:00 and 18:00 PST error rate was ~3.5%, error rates have since decreased to 0.5%.
GCS requests originating in Europe experienced a 17% error rate. Elevated latency and error rates for GCS in Europe.
Stackdriver Uptime Monitoring experienced a period of unavailability
Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router experiencing packet loss in multiple regions
UNKNOWN
errors when viewing clusters on the dashboard
Stackdriver Logging Service Degraded
Support Center inaccessible
Support Center inaccessible
Current data indicates traffic to other regions may see approximately 12% packet loss.
UNKNOWN
Cloud Storage is showing inconsistent result for object listing for multi-regional buckets in US
OAuth login issues
Google Cloud Engine VM instances losing connectivity
users are unable to create new support cases
We are investigating loss for traffic to and from South America.
App Engine Admin-API experiencing high error rates
some instance maybe failing to create or HA instances may reports incorrect 'Unable to Failover' state
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
UNKNOWN
Google BigQuery job failures returning an 'Internal Error' message. Google BigQuery is currently experiencing an elevated rate of job failures.
All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution
Unable to browse GCS storage buckets in Google Console
High latency and failures on queries and deployments.
Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests.
approximately 60% of the Cloud SQL admin operations globally, including instance creation, are affected
UNKNOWN

Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as 'Build failed (internal error)'
Datastore experiencing elevated error rates
Users are unable to create new support cases.
We are experiencing an issue with Memcache availability beginning at November 6, 2017 at 12:30 pm US/Pacific. Current data indicate(s) that all projects using Memcache are affected by this issue.
Customers may experience intermittent elevated errors from the BigQuery API in europe-west2, especially TableService, DatasetService, and JobService operations
UNKNOWN
Google Cloud Function deployments seeing increased errors
Customers may experience intermittent elevated errors from the BigQuery API in europe-west2
Customers without an organization may receive an error message: 'You may not have permissions to create projects in this organization'.
Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router experiencing packet loss in multiple regions

Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud VPN, and Google Cloud Network Load Balancers hosted in the us-east4 region experienced 100% packet loss from the internet and other GCP regions. Google Dedicated Interconnect Attachments located in us-east4 also experienced loss of connectivity.
Google Kubernetes Engine may clear certain add-ons from the configuration UI after a cluster upgrade.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Issue affecting the ability to list projects and organizations
preventing the creation of new GCLB load balancers and updating of existing ones
Customers affected by this issue may see an error similar to the following: ERROR: (gcloud.functions.deploy) OperationError: code=3, message=Build failed
Visible decrease in Cloud Router data in Metrics Explorer.
All the components that require access to storage infrastructure may be affected by this issue in multiple regions.
Autoscaling of instances in Managed Instance Groups may not occur due to instances being unreachable.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Customers are not able to create new databases in Cloud Firestore.
Customers peering with us-west2 may experience packet loss.
Google BigQuery job failures returning an 'Internal Error' message
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
We are experiencing an issue with increased system lag in some Google Cloud Dataflow jobs. Google Cloud Dataflow jobs experiencing system lag and stuck Dataflow jobs.
Affected customers may experience an elevated error rate (up to 100%) when submitting dataset insertion operations.
Google BigQuery users experiencing elevated latency and error rates in US multi-region.
Customers may experience increased latency or connection timeouts between GCP and other Cloud Providers.
Affected customers may experience timeout errors upon cluster creation and deletion. intermittent issue with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation & deletion operations timing out
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Firestore
All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution.
Cloud SQL instances in europe-west2-a may be unreachable
We are investigating an issue with Google Kubernetes Engine node pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
reduced URLFetch availability
Customers may observe their calico node pods failing to start and crash-looping indefinitely on GKE 1.24.7+ and 1.25.3+.

Customers are unable to provision a new instance using Pantheon UI, the region field will not populate. Affected customers were unable to create new instances, as the region field in the user interface (UI) could not be populated due to a metadata issue.
Cloud Functions Gen 2 customers are missing metrics and experiencing incorrect billing for their SKUs.

100 % of customers running "yum" in RHEL instances received a 403 - Forbidden response. All Red Hat instances attempting to get updates encountered failures for the duration of the outage. Customers using our RedHat licenses are currently unable to update/apply RedHat patches.
instances update/export failures
Customers may have experienced up to 40% packet loss between VMs in the following affected regions. Customers may have observed packet loss for transit traffic in the above mentioned cloud regions.
ssh fails with ERROR: (gcloud.compute.start-iap-tunnel) Error while connecting [4047: 'Failed to lookup instance']
pods stuck Terminating or Pending
Customer can observe higher number of failures (ERROR: PERMISSION_DENIED: The caller does not have permission) when trying to list/describe the OAuth client via gCloud or Terraform
Customers may see failures while attempting to update/export their basic tier instances.

Customers will experiences increased error rates. When listing the deployed models in Endpoints, the list will be empty and Predict and Explain requests would fail. All pre-existing Vertex models undeployed on Vertex AI Endpoints. Runtime exceptions and general errors on Predict and Explain requests. Quota failure when trying to re-deploy models.
Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

customers were unable to create or edit Google Compute Engine (GCE) instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error 'Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].'

Some users are experiencing connectivity issues between VMs or with onprem. Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions. Customers may have faced issues with traffic routing.

Customer's might experience following: 1) External HTTP/S Load Balancing, Cloud CDN - config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate 2) Cloud Armor rules may also be affected 3) Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy 4) Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate 5) VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected

Customer may experience replication failure on the new CSA rollout on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts for the following services : - Cloud Load Balancing - Cloud Interconnect - Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Cloud NAT - Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors. Cloud Run deployments timing out and failing.

Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation. Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories receiving failure message 'Request contains an invalid argument.' Affected customers were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters.
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors
Number of Airflow workers in the Composer environment won't change.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased packet loss for egress traffic from Google to the Middle East, and elevated latency between our Europe and Asia Regions. Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts.

Customer are unable to make cloud armour config changes globally. Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes.

Delete function operations are erroring with following message although the corresponding resources are getting deleted and customers are able to reuse the function name. Error message: Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.
Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation
All cloud customers communicating outside the region (including to the internet) would have seen two periods of disruption, ~8m at 04:30 AM, ~10m at 04:48 AM US/Pacific

Customers will be getting IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT in the Asian regions for Google Cloud Storage. Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation for Google Kubernetes Engine. Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR Artifact Registry. Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories, receiving a failure message of 'Request contains an invalid argument.' Affected customers were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters.
ListInstances API requests failed with error code 429. Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.

CPU Load Metrics are impacted and could show the CPU Load as zero in the impacted locations. Customers who are periodically scaling their clusters based on the CPU Load Metrics will be operating on wrong data. Customers may be missing data in Cloud Monitoring for the following metrics: bigtable.googleapis.com/cluster/cpu_load, bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/latency, bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/max_delay
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors
Retry Build operations may fail with an error and the build will not be retried
Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation

Customers will be getting IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT in the Asian regions for Google Cloud Storage. Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation (GKE Cluster Server receives invalid_argument error when creating a GCS bucket as one step of cluster creation) for Google Kubernetes Engine. Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR Artifact Registry
503 error response can be observed for Google Cloud Functions. Cloud Scheduler jobs are failing due to 503 error from Google Cloud Functions.
Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error
Customers who are deploying GCFv2 functions and who have never enabled Cloud Run API before will observe failures with deployments.

Customers may see older jobs (finished more than 180 days ago) and jobs which are currently running disappear from the Console and API output. Jobs which are currently running will see data plane errors if the control plane job resource is deleted.
Datastore customers will experience increased errors and latency.
Customers using Local SSD on VMs could see the VM terminated unexpectedly, which shows in the console as an Instance Termination by Compute Engine.
Customers might have experienced issues while creating new forwarding rules for Global Layer 7 External load balancer or edit or delete existing ones.
Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
